element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Encounters while traveling between populated areas
Can permanently fail quest lines by killing NPCs; inn mechanic resets minor crimes
Behind forest, requires deliberate seeking; signaled by NPC dialogue
Northern mountains, sleeping in cave; signaled by serfs' dialogue; endgame-level threat
Entering unleashes difficulty spike; permanently changes world state

element_introduction_order.element: Magic items (three required for quest resolution)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Including teleportation scroll and Mjolnir-based returning axe

typical_tension_triggers: NPC dialogue revealing consequences (killing important characters)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Open-world exploration with player-paced tension; optional danger zones allow self-regulated difficulty; permanent consequences for NPC deaths create cautious engagement during social encounters

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: NPC dialogue hints at hidden locations (dragon rumors, Treela behind forest); serfs speak of threats in cardinal directions
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden city of Treela located behind forest in map center; dragon location in northern mountains not immediately apparent

difficulty_curve.early_game: Safe introduction via character selection and dialogue-driven interaction; described as 'introductory-level' RPG with simplified menu-based systems
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate tension: exploration introduces combat encounters; optional zones (Treela) signal mid-game content; resource management begins
difficulty_curve.late_game: Escalating danger: dragon encounter signals end-game challenge; Catacombs provide permanent difficulty spike if entered; three magic items required for quest resolution
notable_spikes: Entering Catacombs (unleashes ghouls, permanently increases world difficulty)
Dragon encounter in northern mountains (endgame boss-equivalent threat)
Realizing critical NPCs are permanently killable (raises stakes of all social encounters)

notable_level_patterns.description: 13,000 screens of continuous map with no loading screens; overwhelming scale supports exploration-driven gameplay; day/night cycle color changes world dynamically

Catacombs and northern dragon cave are discoverable but not mandatory; entering either creates permanent world state changes (ghoul release = difficulty spike); signals progression through NPC dialogue
Treela city hidden behind forest in map center; requires inference from dialogue or exploration; rewards deliberate thoroughness
Towns and populated areas feature NPCs with quest-critical roles; killing them creates unwinnable states; inn mechanic allows forgiveness of minor crimes but not major NPC deaths
